//: Allowlist for backend/scripts/check-audit.js. Each entry must have a GHSA id, a reason, an addedOn date, and ideally a sunsetBy date. Re-review on every release.

advisories.title: Prototype Pollution in sheetJS
advisories.reason: No fix available upstream — SheetJS community edition is unmaintained vs the paid CDN-hosted version. We use xlsx for one-shot CSV/Excel imports in routes/expenses + routes/contacts. The Prototype Pollution vector requires attacker-controlled input to xlsx.read(); our code only parses files uploaded via authenticated admin routes, mitigating the risk. Long-term: replace with exceljs (CI-4 follow-up).

advisories.title: SheetJS Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS)
advisories.reason: Same package as above; same mitigation (auth-gated input). Same long-term fix (replace xlsx with exceljs).

advisories.title: semver vulnerable to Regular Expression Denial of Service
advisories.reason: Transitive: imap@0.8.19 → utf7 → semver@2.x. Fix requires downgrading imap to 0.8.17 (semver-major) or replacing imap entirely with node-imap-simple / nodemailer's IMAP client. The IMAP feature is used by routes/email_inbound.js for Mailgun reply scraping; ReDoS surface is bounded to malformed UTF-7-encoded mailbox names from the IMAP server, which is server-controlled (Mailgun) and not attacker-controlled. Long-term: migrate off imap (CI-4 follow-up).

advisories.title: Transitive: depends on vulnerable utf7 → semver
advisories.reason: Same root cause as the semver entry above. npm audit reports imap as a separate top-level entry with no direct GHSA. Allowlisted by package name in check-audit.js's transitive-root path.

advisories.title: Transitive: depends on vulnerable semver
advisories.reason: Same root cause as the semver entry above. Allowlisted by package name.

advisories.title: path-to-regexp vulnerable to Regular Expression Denial of Service via multiple route parameters
advisories.reason: Transitive via Express. `npm audit fix` couldn't auto-resolve under our current Express major. Risk window is route-parameter parsing where an attacker can craft a URL with adversarial pattern segments. We don't expose dynamic route patterns — every Express route uses simple `:id`-style params. ReDoS surface is bounded to path matching, mitigated by Cloudflare's request-time limits in front of the demo. Long-term: upgrade Express to 4.21.2+ when a non-breaking path becomes available.

advisories.title: sanitize-html allows misuse of disallowedTagsMode bypassing sanitization
advisories.reason: Surfaced after PR #769 + #819 brought in new transitive deps that re-pinned the vulnerable sanitize-html 2.x line. The CVE allows a caller passing a crafted `disallowedTagsMode` option value to bypass tag stripping. Our two callsites (backend/middleware/security.js sanitizeBody + backend/lib/sanitizeJson.js) build their config object from literals under our own control — neither accepts user-supplied options. Both run AFTER express.json() parses the request body, so an attacker cannot inject an options object via the wire. No fix available in 2.x; upstream 3.x is a breaking API change. Long-term: upgrade to sanitize-html 3.x in a focused PR that re-validates every callsite.
